---
title: "Quarto Exercises Reference"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark: darkly
filters:
  - quarto-exercises
---

This page showcases how to configure and structure interactive practice exercises with the `quarto-exercises` extension.

## Configuration

Place configuration overrides in the document frontmatter under `quarto-exercises`. Options not specified will fall back to their system defaults.

```yaml
quarto-exercises:
  instant: false              # Validate answers on change instead of waiting for a Check button
  reveal: false               # Reveal correct answers and statuses when checking
  lock: false                 # Disable input fields after a correct answer is checked
  reset: true                 # Show a Reset button to clear student inputs
  shuffle: false              # Randomize the display order of choices
  reshuffle-on-reset: false   # Randomize option order again when clicking Reset
  show-answers: false         # Print answers inline or below questions (mostly for print/PDF)
  explanation: correct        # When to reveal explanations: correct, after-check, or never
  feedback-correct: "Correct!" # Message displayed next to controls on a correct answer
  feedback-incorrect: "Not quite." # Message displayed next to controls on an incorrect answer
  ignore-case: false          # Ignore casing during text input validation
```

---

## Multiple Choice Exercises

```markdown
::: {.exercise}
Who carried the One Ring out of the Shire?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer}
Boromir
:::

::: {.answer}
Legolas Greenleaf
:::
:::
```

::: {.exercise}
Who carried the One Ring out of the Shire?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer}
Boromir
:::

::: {.answer}
Legolas Greenleaf
:::
:::

---

```markdown
::: {.exercise shuffle=true}
Which members of the Fellowship are hobbits? Select all that apply.

::: {.answer correct=true key="frodo"}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer key="legolas"}
Legolas Greenleaf
:::

::: {.answer correct=true key="sam"}
Samwise Gamgee
:::
:::
```

::: {.exercise shuffle=true}
Which members of the Fellowship are hobbits? Select all that apply.

::: {.answer correct=true key="frodo"}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer key="legolas"}
Legolas Greenleaf
:::

::: {.answer correct=true key="sam"}
Samwise Gamgee
:::
:::

---

```markdown
::: {.exercise}
What is the name of the wizard who guides the Fellowship?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Gandalf
:::

::: {.answer}
Saruman
:::

::: {.explanation}
Gandalf is one of the Istari (wizards) sent to Middle-earth to oppose Sauron.
:::

::: {.hint}
He is also known as Mithrandir.
:::
:::
```

::: {.exercise}
What is the name of the wizard who guides the Fellowship?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Gandalf
:::

::: {.answer}
Saruman
:::

::: {.explanation}
Gandalf is one of the Istari (wizards) sent to Middle-earth to oppose Sauron.
:::

::: {.hint}
He is also known as Mithrandir.
:::
:::

---

## Inline Blanks

```markdown
The Capital of Gondor is [`Minas Tirith`]{.blank answer="Minas Tirith"}.
```

The Capital of Gondor is [`Minas Tirith`]{.blank answer="Minas Tirith"}.

---

```markdown
The Ringbearer is [`Frodo`]{.blank answers="Frodo,Frodo Baggins" ignore-case=true}.
```

The Ringbearer is [`Frodo`]{.blank answers="Frodo,Frodo Baggins" ignore-case=true}.

---

```markdown
Enter the Fellowship member count in binary (binary representation of 9): [`1001`]{.blank answer="^1001$" match="regex"}.
```

Enter the Fellowship member count in binary (binary representation of 9): [`1001`]{.blank answer="^1001$" match="regex"}.

---

```markdown
Name the hobbit who accompanied Frodo to Mount Doom: [`Sam`]{.blank answers="Samwise,Sam,Samwise Gamgee" match="one-of" ignore-case=true trim=true collapse-space=true feedback-correct="Yes, Samwise!" feedback-incorrect="Try again. Think of Frodo's loyal gardener."}.
```

Name the hobbit who accompanied Frodo to Mount Doom: [`Sam`]{.blank answers="Samwise,Sam,Samwise Gamgee" match="one-of" ignore-case=true trim=true collapse-space=true feedback-correct="Yes, Samwise!" feedback-incorrect="Try again. Think of Frodo's loyal gardener."}.

---

## Inline Dropdowns

```markdown
The One Ring was forged in [Mordor / Gondor / Rohan]{.choose answer="Mordor"}.
```

The One Ring was forged in [Mordor / Gondor / Rohan]{.choose answer="Mordor"}.

---

```markdown
The capital of Rohan is [Edoras / Helm's Deep / Isengard]{.choose answer="Edoras" shuffle=true feedback-incorrect="Not the correct location."}.
```

The capital of Rohan is [Edoras / Helm's Deep / Isengard]{.choose answer="Edoras" shuffle=true feedback-incorrect="Not the correct location."}.

---

## Shared Controls Exercise

```markdown
::: {.exercise}
The hobbits are saved at the Prancing Pony by [Aragorn / Boromir / Legolas / Gimli]{.choose answer="Aragorn"}, who is also known as [Strider]{.blank answer="Strider"}.
:::
```

::: {.exercise}
The hobbits are saved at the Prancing Pony by [Aragorn / Boromir / Legolas / Gimli]{.choose answer="Aragorn"}, who is also known as [Strider]{.blank answer="Strider"}.
:::

## Code Cloze

Use `.code-cloze` blocks to place interactive blanks and dropdowns directly inside syntax-highlighted code. Wrap the targets in double curly braces `{{` and `}}`. Standalone `.code-cloze` blocks render with their own Check and Reset controls.

### Standalone Code Cloze

```markdown
```{.code-cloze lang="r"}
# Select the correct R collection type and complete calculations
x <- {{choose answer="c" options="c,list,data.frame"}}(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
total <- {{blank answer="sum"}}(x)
cat("Total:", total, "\n")
```
```

```{.code-cloze lang="r"}
# Select the correct R collection type and complete calculations
x <- {{choose answer="c" options="c,list,data.frame"}}(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
total <- {{blank answer="sum"}}(x)
cat("Total:", total, "\n")
```

---

### Nested in a Parent Exercise

Wrapping a `.code-cloze` block in an `.exercise` Div links it to parent Check and Reset controls, allowing custom feedback overrides, hints, and explanations.

```markdown
::: {.exercise explanation="after-check"}
Complete the Python list comprehension program:

```{.code-cloze lang="python"}
# Filter elements using list comprehension
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
total = {{choose answer="sum" options="sum,max,min,len"}}(numbers)
print({{blank answer="total"}})
```

::: {.explanation}
The `sum()` function returns the total sum of all items in an iterable.
:::

::: {.hint}
Think of list aggregation functions.
:::
:::
```

::: {.exercise explanation="after-check"}
Complete the Python list comprehension program:

```{.code-cloze lang="python"}
# Filter elements using list comprehension
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
total = {{choose answer="sum" options="sum,max,min,len"}}(numbers)
print({{blank answer="total"}})
```

::: {.explanation}
The `sum()` function returns the total sum of all items in an iterable.
:::

::: {.hint}
Think of list aggregation functions.
:::
:::
